clusters.title: Miracle on Mount Everest
clusters.neutral_headline: Sherpa found alive on Mount Everest after six days missing
bullet_summary: A remarkable story on Mount Everest…after being missing for nearly a week, a Sherpa is found crawling back to base camp
A Nepali Sherpa has been found alive on the slopes of Mount Everest after missing for six days without food or oxygen
Cleaners found Dawa Sherpa crawling towards Base Camp six days after he went missing at a higher altitude

items.title: Miracle on Mount Everest
items.standfirst: A remarkable story on Mount Everest…after being missing for nearly a week, a Sherpa is found crawling back to base camp. He describes how he defied the odds and survived. Janis Mackey Frayer has his story.
items.content: A remarkable story on Mount Everest…after being missing for nearly a week, a Sherpa is found crawling back to base camp. He describes how he defied the odds and survived. Janis Mackey Frayer has his story.

items.title: Sherpa found alive on Mount Everest after six days missing
items.standfirst: A Nepali Sherpa has been found alive on the slopes of Mount Everest after missing for six days without food or oxygen.
items.content: A Nepali Sherpa has been found alive on the slopes of Mount Everest after missing for six days without food or oxygen.

items.title: Missing Sherpa guide found alive on Everest after funeral rites had begun
items.standfirst: Climbing support team rescue Hillary Dawa Sherpa almost a week on from when he was last seenA Nepali guide who was believed to have died on Mount Everest has been found crawling to base camp a week after going missing – and after his funeral rites had begun.Dawa Sherpa, also known as Hillary Dawa Sherpa after the famous climber Edmund Hillary, was last seen on 29 May but did not reach base camp with other climbing groups. Continue reading...
items.content: Climbing support team rescue Hillary Dawa Sherpa almost a week on from when he was last seenA Nepali guide who was believed to have died on Mount Everest has been found crawling to base camp a week after going missing – and after his funeral rites had begun.Dawa Sherpa, also known as Hillary Dawa Sherpa after the famous climber Edmund Hillary, was last seen on 29 May but did not reach base camp with other climbing groups. Continue reading...

items.title: Missing Sherpa crawls back to Mount Everest base camp
items.standfirst: A Sherpa guide has crawled back to base camp on Mount Everest a week after going missing.
items.content: A Sherpa guide has crawled back to base camp on Mount Everest a week after going missing.

items.title: Missing Sherpa guide found on Mount Everest after 'miracle' self-rescue
items.standfirst: Cleaners found Dawa Sherpa crawling towards Base Camp six days after he went missing at a higher altitude.
items.content: Cleaners found Dawa Sherpa crawling towards Base Camp six days after he went missing at a higher altitude.

items.title: Marjane Satrapi, French-Iranian author of Persepolis, dies at 56
items.standfirst: French-Iranian author and illustrator Marjane Satrapi, best known for Persepolis, has died at 56, according to members of her entourage. The graphic memoir became an acclaimed book and film. [france24.com]
items.content: French-Iranian author and illustrator Marjane Satrapi, best known for Persepolis, has died at 56, according to members of her entourage. The graphic memoir became an acclaimed book and film. [france24.com]

items.title: Zelensky proposes face-to-face talks in open letter to Putin
items.standfirst: Ukraine's president tells the Russian leader that only "direct engagement" between the two countries could end the war, with the US focused on Iran.
items.content: Ukraine's president tells the Russian leader that only "direct engagement" between the two countries could end the war, with the US focused on Iran.

items.title: Zelenskyy calls for face-to-face Ukraine war negotiations in letter to Putin
items.standfirst: Ukrainian president proposes meeting in neutral third country as Trump says both sides have to ‘make compromises’The Ukrainian president, Volodymyr Zelenskyy, has called for face-to-face negotiations in a public letter addressed directly to the Russian president, Vladimir Putin.The letter, the first Zelenskyy has publicly written directly to Putin since Russia launched its full-scale invasion in 2022, was a sweeping criticism of the Russian leader’s 26 years in power. Continue reading...
items.content: Ukrainian president proposes meeting in neutral third country as Trump says both sides have to ‘make compromises’The Ukrainian president, Volodymyr Zelenskyy, has called for face-to-face negotiations in a public letter addressed directly to the Russian president, Vladimir Putin.The letter, the first Zelenskyy has publicly written directly to Putin since Russia launched its full-scale invasion in 2022, was a sweeping criticism of the Russian leader’s 26 years in power. Continue reading...

items.title: Zelensky in open letter to Putin proposes face-to-face meeting and 'full ceasefire'
items.standfirst: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Thursday called for a face-to-face meeting with his Russian counterpart Vladimir Putin in a rare open letter, saying he was also ready for a “full ceasefire”. In response, the Kremlin said Zelensky could meet Putin in Moscow "any time".
items.content: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Thursday called for a face-to-face meeting with his Russian counterpart Vladimir Putin in a rare open letter, saying he was also ready for a “full ceasefire”. In response, the Kremlin said Zelensky could meet Putin in Moscow "any time".

items.title: How shock French Open finalist's tiny tattoo gives meaning to big run
items.standfirst: Qualifier Maja Chwalinska is one win away from an unthinkable French Open title after coming through the darkest period of her life.
items.content: Qualifier Maja Chwalinska is one win away from an unthinkable French Open title after coming through the darkest period of her life.

items.title: History-making qualifier Chwalinska sets up Andreeva French Open final
items.standfirst: Poland's Maja Chwalinska beats Diana Shnaider to reach French Open final and makes history after qualifying run.
items.content: Poland's Maja Chwalinska beats Diana Shnaider to reach French Open final and makes history after qualifying run.

items.title: Polish qualifier Maja Chwalinska carries fairytale run into French Open final
items.standfirst: Polish world number 114 Maja Chwalinska made French Open history on Thursday as she beat Russian Diana Shnaider in straight sets to become the first qualifier to go all the way to the final at Roland Garros – and only the second in Grand Slam history after Emma Raducanu's title run at the US Open in 2021.
items.content: Polish world number 114 Maja Chwalinska made French Open history on Thursday as she beat Russian Diana Shnaider in straight sets to become the first qualifier to go all the way to the final at Roland Garros – and only the second in Grand Slam history after Emma Raducanu's title run at the US Open in 2021.

items.title: First officer told captain of United Airlines flight that hit light pole he was "slow and a little low," NTSB says

items.content: The National Transportation Safety Board released its preliminary report on a United Airlines plane that struck a light pole on the New Jersey Turnpike in May.

items.title: New look at United Airlines incident involving truck on New Jersey highway
items.standfirst: Last month, a United Airlines jet hit a light pole and damaged a bakery truck as it was coming in for landing at Newark Liberty International Airport. The NTSB has been investigating and released its first preliminary report on Thursday. Tom Hanson has more.
items.content: Last month, a United Airlines jet hit a light pole and damaged a bakery truck as it was coming in for landing at Newark Liberty International Airport. The NTSB has been investigating and released its first preliminary report on Thursday. Tom Hanson has more.

items.title: NTSB says United jet was too slow and too low in Newark landing accident
items.standfirst: Federal investigators say the captain flying the United 767 from Italy was too slow and too low before landing last month at Newark, N.J. The jet struck a light pole, damaging a truck on the turnpike.
items.content: Federal investigators say the captain flying the United 767 from Italy was too slow and too low before landing last month at Newark, N.J. The jet struck a light pole, damaging a truck on the turnpike.(Image credit: Joshua A. Bickel)

items.title: Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner rejects new allegations of abusive behavior
items.standfirst: Platner says claims in New York Times article of physical misconduct and offensive remarks ‘politically motivated’Graham Platner, a Democratic candidate for the US Senate, has rejected an explosive new report about his treatment of women, insisting that allegations of abusive behavior are “politically motivated”.Platner, a progressive running for election in Maine, was responding to a New York Times article published on Thursday that included an interview with a Republican operative who accused him of womanizing, physical misconduct and making troubling comments about rape. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

clusters.title: 'Judicial system should believe victims first', activist says as body found in missing girl case
clusters.neutral_headline: Outrage in France as body discovered in case of missing 11‑year‑old Lyhanna
bullet_summary: A body was discovered on Thursday and formal identification was under way, an informed source said
Reported by France24, Guardian World

items.title: 'Judicial system should believe victims first', activist says as body found in missing girl case
items.standfirst: Outrage has erupted in France after it emerged the main suspect in the case of an 11-year-old girl missing since last week had been repeatedly accused of sexually abusing children with no action taken. A body was discovered on Thursday and formal identification was underway. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney, Mié Kohiyama, co-president of 'Be Brave France', says that "the judicial system should consider first and believe the victims and the survivors".
items.content: Outrage has erupted in France after it emerged the main suspect in the case of an 11-year-old girl missing since last week had been repeatedly accused of sexually abusing children with no action taken. A body was discovered on Thursday and formal identification was underway. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney, Mié Kohiyama, co-president of 'Be Brave France', says that "the judicial system should consider first and believe the victims and the survivors".

items.title: Outrage in France as body discovered in case of missing 11‑year‑old Lyhanna
items.standfirst: Outrage erupted in France on Thursday after a body was discovered in the search for 11-year-old schoolgirl Lyhanna and it was revealed that the key suspect had been repeatedly accused of sexually abusing children but no action had been taken. The body has yet to be formally identified.
items.content: Outrage erupted in France on Thursday after a body was discovered in the search for 11-year-old schoolgirl Lyhanna and it was revealed that the key suspect had been repeatedly accused of sexually abusing children but no action had been taken. The body has yet to be formally identified.

Continue reading...

clusters.title: Lebanon health ministry says Israeli strikes in south and east kill eight
clusters.neutral_headline: Hezbollah rejects Israel-Lebanon truce as Trump scrambles to end Iran war
bullet_summary: An Israeli strike in eastern Lebanon killed five people on Thursday, while another attack near the southern city of Tyre killed three more, the Lebanese health ministry said
Reported by France24, Guardian World

items.title: Lebanon health ministry says Israeli strikes in south and east kill eight
items.standfirst: An Israeli strike in eastern Lebanon killed five people on Thursday, while another attack near the southern city of Tyre killed three more, the Lebanese health ministry said. The strikes also left eight wounded, including three children and two women. They came a day after the Lebanese and Israeli governments agreed a ceasefire plan for Israel's war with Hezbollah, which the group has rejected. FRANCE 24's Antonia Kerrigan reports from Beirut.
items.content: An Israeli strike in eastern Lebanon killed five people on Thursday, while another attack near the southern city of Tyre killed three more, the Lebanese health ministry said. The strikes also left eight wounded, including three children and two women. They came a day after the Lebanese and Israeli governments agreed a ceasefire plan for Israel's war with Hezbollah, which the group has rejected. FRANCE 24's Antonia Kerrigan reports from Beirut.

items.title: Hezbollah rejects Israel-Lebanon truce as Trump scrambles to end Iran war
items.standfirst: Group calls ceasefire a ‘roadmap to annihilate part of the Lebanese people’, throwing regional peace talks into doubtHezbollah has rejected a US-brokered ceasefire plan agreed by the Lebanese and Israeli governments, throwing the future of a truce in Lebanon and regional peace negotiations into question.The group’s leader, Naim Qassem, called the plan a “roadmap to annihilate part of the Lebanese people” in a statement on Thursday. Continue reading...
items.content: Group calls ceasefire a ‘roadmap to annihilate part of the Lebanese people’, throwing regional peace talks into doubtHezbollah has rejected a US-brokered ceasefire plan agreed by the Lebanese and Israeli governments, throwing the future of a truce in Lebanon and regional peace negotiations into question.The group’s leader, Naim Qassem, called the plan a “roadmap to annihilate part of the Lebanese people” in a statement on Thursday. clusters.title: Albania: luxury complex project linked to Trump's family sparks outrage
clusters.neutral_headline: Kushner-backed luxury resort plan sparks protests in Albania
bullet_summary: Protests started end of May after videos showed bulldozers on the #beach
Reported by France24, BBC World

items.title: Albania: luxury complex project linked to Trump's family sparks outrage
items.standfirst: Anger is growing in #Albania over a plan to build luxury hotels and villas in a protected area on the #Adriatic coast. The project is being led by an investment #fund linked to #Trump's son-in-law Jared #Kushner. Protests started end of May after videos showed bulldozers on the #beach.
items.content: Anger is growing in #Albania over a plan to build luxury hotels and villas in a protected area on the #Adriatic coast. The project is being led by an investment #fund linked to #Trump's son-in-law Jared #Kushner. Protests started end of May after videos showed bulldozers on the #beach.
